items.headline: U.S. Futures Point Lower as Geopolitical Tensions Escalate
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are broadly lower this Monday, March 30, 2026, as persistent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East continue to drive risk aversion and a surge in global oil prices. The market enters a holiday-shortened week with caution, following a challenging previous week where major indices extended their losing streaks, with the Dow and Nasdaq officially entering correction territory.

items.statContext: S&P 500 futures were down approximately 0.06% in early trading on March 30, 2026, amid rising global concerns. The S&P 500 index closed Friday, March 27, down 1.7% at 6,368.85, marking its worst week since the Iran conflict began.
NASDAQ 100 futures dipped around 0.13% in early Monday trading. On Friday, March 27, the Nasdaq composite sank 2.1% to 20,948.36, officially placing it in correction territory.
Dow Jones Industrial Average futures were down roughly 0.11% as of early March 30. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.7% on Friday, March 27, to 45,166.64, entering correction territory.

items.headline: Entergy Corporation Leads Gainers
items.summary: Entergy Corporation was among the top S&P 500 gainers on Friday, March 27, reflecting potential defensive positioning amidst broader market uncertainty.

items.headline: Campbell's Company Shows Strength
items.summary: The Campbell's Company saw a notable rise on Friday, March 27, as investors sought stability in consumer staples.

items.headline: Halliburton Rises with Oil Prices
items.summary: Halliburton Company climbed on Friday, March 27, likely benefiting from the continued surge in crude oil prices driven by Middle East tensions.

items.headline: Datadog Among Top Losers
items.summary: Datadog, Inc. experienced a significant decline on Friday, March 27, as technology stocks faced renewed pressure in a risk-off environment.

items.headline: Moderna Falls Sharply
items.summary: Moderna, Inc. was a significant loser on Friday, March 27, as broader market fears impacted growth-oriented sectors.

items.headline: Coinbase Global Declines
items.summary: Coinbase Global, Inc. saw a considerable drop on Friday, March 27, amidst a wider sell-off in riskier assets.

items.headline: Energy Sector Shows Resilience Amid Tech Headwinds
items.summary: In the broader market, the energy sector has shown relative resilience, while technology and consumer discretionary sectors have led losses. This indicates a rotation away from growth-oriented stocks as investors prioritize defensive plays. PSU banks in India also experienced significant plunges.

items.headline: Key Economic Data and Earnings to Watch This Week
items.summary: Investors are closely awaiting crucial U.S. labor market data this week, including the Nonfarm Payrolls report and the ISM Purchasing Managers' Index. Earlier data showed that U.S. consumer sentiment deteriorated more than previously estimated in March. On the earnings front, major companies such as Nike ($NKE), McCormick & Company ($MKC), and Conagra Brands ($CAG) are scheduled to report results. Additionally, Oxbridge Re Holdings Ltd ($OXBR) and INmune Bio ($INMB) are hosting their Q4 2025 earnings calls today, March 30, 2026.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Continues to Drive Market Uncertainty and Oil Prices
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, now in its fifth week, remains a dominant macro trend affecting global markets, fueling risk aversion and significant increases in crude oil prices. Brent crude has soared to around $115-116 per barrel. This prolonged uncertainty is prompting investors to reconsider the long-term economic implications, contributing to the recent market downturn.
